original: Arab Muslim army sent by Caliph Omar begins siege of Heliopolis, city succumbs mid to late July, paving way for Muslim conquest of Byzantine Egypt
German King Henry II the Saint crowned
Twenty-four wagonloads of Talmudic books are burned in Paris
Franciscan theologian William of Ockham [Occam] excommunicated by Pope John XXII
Inhabitants of Seville, Spain, massacre 5,000 Jews
Battle of Novara, the War of the League of Cambrai: the Swiss Confederacy defeat the French
France and England sign treaty of Scotland
Mexico begins its inquisition
Massachusetts grants 500 acres of land to erect a gunpowder mill
Queen Christina of Sweden resigns and converts to Catholicism
France and Brandenburg sign peace treaty
The Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, England, opens as the world's first university museum
French transport the first African slaves to Louisiana
France and Prussia sign peace treaty
Third great fire in Moscow in two weeks; one-third of the city is destroyed
Haitian explorer Jean Baptiste-Pointe Dusable settles in Chicago
Franklin College founded, Lancaster, Pennsylvania. Now part of Franklin & Marshall College.
Fire destroys one-third of Copenhagen, injuring 18,000 people
Peace of Badajoz: Spain-Portugal
Sweden declares independence, constitutional monarchy established with a new constitution empowering Riksdag after 20 years of absolute monarchy
US invasion of Canada halted at Stoney Creek (Ontario)
2nd US national black convention (Philadelphia)
The barricades fall and the Paris student uprisings of 1832 end
The Philadelphia, Germantown and Norristown Railroad opens its  initial line from Philadelphia to Germantown, Pennsylvania; run is 6 miles (9.7 km) long and creates first US 'railroad suburb'
New York Yacht Club holds its first annual regatta; won by the schooner Carnelia
Queensland, Australia, is established as a separate colony from New South Wales, celebrated as Queensland Day
Lincoln's cabinet declares Union government will pay for expenses once states have mobilized volunteers
Battle of Memphis fought on the Mississippi River, Union forces defeat Confederate fleet leading to the city's surrender
Skirmish at Harrisonburg, Virginia, sees the Confederates win a minor victory in the Battle of Good's Farm
Battle of Milliken's Bend, Louisiana and Williamsport, Maryland
Battle of Lake Chicot, Arkansas (Dutch Bayou)
Netherlands joins the gold standard
Cyclone in Arabian Sea (Bombay, India) drowns 100,000 people (disputed event)
Electric iron is patented by New York inventor Henry W. Seely; it weighs almost 15 pounds and is very slow to heat up

Ethiopia: Shewan forces of Menelik defeat the Gojjame army in the Battle of Embabo. The Shewans capture Negus Tekle Haymanot of Gojjam, their victory leads to a Shewan hegemony over the territories south of the Abay River.
Great Fire in Seattle destroys 25 downtown blocks
United States Polo Association forms in NYC
Chicago South Side Elevated Railroad opens its first 3.6 miles
Frank Samuelsen and George Harbo leave NY harbor to row across Atlantic; their 55 day record for rowing was not broken for 114 years
Boxers cut off all railroad links between Peking and Tientsin, main port city of Peking
US Congress pass an act authorizing a civil code and government for the territory of Alaska after gold discoveries bring lawlessness and disorder to the area
President Emile Loubet of France and Minister of Foreign Affairs Theophile Declasse visit London, furthering the cause of Entente Cordiale between Britain and France
National Tuberculosis Association organized, Atlantic City, New Jersey
French Foreign minister Delcasse resigns on German request
Paris Métro Line 5 is inaugurated with a first section from Place d'Italie to the Gare d'Orléans (today known as Gare d'Austerlitz)
Nicaragua signs treaty turning over customs to US (not ratified)
Rabbit Maranville, is thrown out trying to steal home 3 times
First air flight out of sight of land (Scotland to Norway)
Voters in East Cleveland, Ohio approve women suffrage
Battle of Belleau Wood, 1st US victory of WW I
Assent is given to an Act to amend the Canadian Currency Act, 1910
Finland declares war on bolsheviks
The Republic of Prekmurje ends
Baron Pyotr Nikolayevich Wrangel opens White Russian offensive against red Army
Detroit Stars' Bill Gatewood pitches the 1st no-hitter in Negro League history, defeating the Cuban Stars 4-0
Egyptian government of Adly Pasha forms
"There Ought To Be A Moonlight Saving Time" by Guy Lombardo hits #1
Belgian government of Henri Jaspar falls
NY Yankees turn triple-play but lose 7-5 to Cleveland Indians
Ijsselmeervogels soccer team forms in Spakenburg
The Revenue Act of 1932 is enacted, creating the first gas tax in the United States, at a rate of 1 cent per US gallon (1/4 ¢/L) sold
Richard Hollingshead opens the world's 1st Drive-In movie theater on Admiral Wilson Boulevard in Pennsauken, New Jersey
US Employment Service created
Securities & Exchange Commission established
Yankee Myrl Hoag hits 6 singles in one game
Aviation gasoline 1st produced commercially Paulsboro, New Jersey
Philadelphia Phillies trailing 8-2 to St Louis, forfeit game
Volcanoes Tavurvur and Vulcan destroy the town of Rabaul on New Britain island, Papua, New Guinea, killing over 500 people
NY Giants beat Reds 17-3, with 5 HRs in 4th inning
NY supreme court justice J F Crater legally declared dead

The ship MS St. Louis, carrying 907 Jewish refugees from Europe, begins sailing back to the continent after it was refused entry into America. Approximately a quarter of those on board would perish in the Holocaust.
1st US Navy vessel constructed as mine layer, USS Terror (CM-5) launched from the Philadelphia Navy Yard
MLB New York Giants use plastic batting helmets for 1st time

1st nylon parachute jump is made by Ameican parachute tester Adeline Gray (27-ish), at Brainard Field, an airport near Hartford, Connecticut; Dupont teamed with the Pioneer Parachute Company to develop use of an alternative to silk
Japanese forces retreat, ending Battle of Midway
Japanese troop land on Kiska, Aleutians
Alaska Airlines commences operations
German submarines U-955, U-970, U-629 and U-373 sink in Bay of Biscay
Operation Overlord: D-Day begins as the 156,000-strong Allied Expeditionary Force lands in Normandy, France, during World War II
World War II: All Major League Baseball games are cancelled in honor of the D-Day landings in northern France
"Free People" premieres in Amsterdam
Henry Morgan is 1st to take off shirt on TV
The Basketball Association of America is formed in New York City
Treaty drawn up for establishment of International Patent Institute
Orapin Chaiyakan becomes the first Thai woman to be elected to the Parliament of Thailand
WKY (now KTVY) TV channel 4 in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma (NBC) begins broadcasting
German DR & Poland sign treaty about Oder-Neisse border
Turkey: The Adhan in Arabic is legalized.
MLB Baltimore Orioles manager Jimmy Dykes is ejected from  both games of a doubleheader at Yankee Stadium, NYC
David Marshall, Singapore's first Chief Minister, resigns.
Ozzie Virgil is 1st Black player for MLB Detroit Tigers
South African police kill 11 Pondo's at Nqusa Hill
Gasunie, Dutch gas and transportation company established

The Beatles, with Jimmy Nichol substituting for Ringo, arrive in Netherlands, take a whirlwind boat tour through the canals of Amsterdam, and perform two shows at Veilinghal Op Hoop Van Zegen, an auction hall in the village of Blokker
Under a temporary order, the rocket launches at Cuxhaven, Germany, are terminated, though they never resume
Yankees Tom Tresh bangs 3 consecutive HRs beating White Sox 12-0
Gemini 9 completes 45 orbits after rendezvous with "angry alligator"
Stokely Carmichael launches "Black Power" movement
WKHA TV channel 35 in Hazard, KY (PBS) begins broadcasting
Air West flight 706 collides with a US Marine Corps F-4B Phantom jet over Los Angeles killing all 49 aboard the DC-6 and the pilot of the F-4B
Soyuz 11 takes 3 cosmonauts to Salyut 1 space station
WHAE (now WGNX) TV channel 46 in Atlanta, Georgia (CBN) begins broadcasting
Explosion at world's largest coal mine kills 427 (Wankie, Rhodesia)
Gold hits record $60 an ounce in London
US bombs Haiphong, North-Vietnam; 1000s killed
47th National Spelling Bee: Julie Ann Junkin wins spelling hydrophyte
A new Instrument of Government is promulgated making Sweden a parliamentary monarchy
British voters decide to remain in Common Market
Provisional Revolutionary Government of South Vietnam established
"The Omen" premieres in the UK
Doobie Brothers sponsor a Golf Classic & Concert at Calabasas Park Country Club & Century Plaza Hotel in Los Angeles to benefit the United Way
Joseph L Howze installed as bishop of Roman Catholic diocese (Miss)
Supreme Court tosses out automatic death penalty laws
Proposition 13 cuts California property taxes 57%
200th running of horse's Derby in England
Royal Air Force receives 1st F-16
Seattle Mariners slugger Willie Horton becomes 43rd player to hit 300 HRs in Major League Baseball
A passenger train travelling between Mansi and Saharsa, India, jumps the tracks at a bridge crossing the Bagmati river, killing 268 officially with another 300 or more missing
Maya Yang Lin wins competition to design the Vietnam War Memorial
30,000 Israeli troops invade Lebanon to drive out the PLO
36th Tony Awards: "The Life and Adventures of Nicholas Nickleby" (play) & "Nine" (musical) win
Bernard Glassman installed as abbot of Zen Center of NY
Bottle with note of June 9, 1910 found in Queensland
Minnesota Twins select pitcher Tim Belcher #1 in the 1983 MLB Draft
Nicaragua expels 3 US diplomats
1,200 die in Sikh "Golden Temple" uprising India
Video game Tetris is first released in the Soviet Union by Alexey Pajitnov
58th National Spelling Bee: Balu Natarajan wins spelling milieu
Body of Nazi concentration camp doctor Dr Josef Mengele located and exhumed
Dutch 2nd Chamber accepts "status" of Aruba
Soyuz T-13 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 7 space station
Jurgen Schull sets world discus record (74.07 m)
Kathy Ormsby, a 21-year-old member of NC State track team jumps off a bridge permanently paralyzing herself
NY Yankees play their 13,000th game
3 giant turtles found in Bronx sewage plant
George H. W. Bush makes campaign promise to support reparations for WW II to Japanese-American internees (promise broken, May 1989)
Mets turn their 1st triple play in 7 years but lose to Cubs 8-4
2nd International Rock Awards
Stump Merrill replaces Bucky Dent as NY Yankee manager
Albert Belleis shipped to minors for not running out a ground ball
Dana Plato receives 6 yr suspended sentence for robbing a video store
Test Cricket debut of Graeme Hick, v West Indies at Headingley
America West Arena opens in Phoenix, Arizona; home court of NBA Phoenix Suns

Ben Vereen suffers head and leg injuries when hit by a vehicle while walking along a dark stretch of highway in Malibu, California; Vereen had earlier fallen asleep while driving and hit a tree, record producer David Foster was behind the wheel of vehicle that hit Vereen
"What's Love Got to Do with It", directed by Brian Gibson and starring Angela Bassett, premieres in Hollywood, California
47th Tony Awards: "Angels in America: Millennium Approaches" (play) and "Kiss of the Spider Woman" (musical) win
6th Children's Miracle Network Telethon
Punsalmaagiyn Otsjirbat recognized as President of Mongolia
Ramiro de Leon Carpio elected President of Guatemala
6.0 earthquake followed by avalanche destroys Toez, Colombia (about 1000 killed)
Tupolev-154M crashes at Xian China, 160 killed
Warwickshire score 4 for 810 declared against Durham
Moses Kiptanui of Kenya runs world 5,000m record 12:55.30 in Rome, Italy
53rd Tony Awards: "Fosse" (musical) and "Side Man" (play) win

Largest jailbreak in Brazilian history at the Putim maximum security prison in Brazil, 345 prisoners run from the main gate. In the ensuing manhunt, two fugitives are killed and five innocent bystanders are accidentally jailed.
Sydney Swans Australian Rules Football forward Tony Lockett breaks Gordon Coventry's VFL/AFL career goals record with 1,300th major early in 145-94 win v Collingwood at the SCG

A near-Earth asteroid estimated at 10 meters in diameter explodes over the Mediterranean Sea between Greece and Libya, with the resulting explosion estimated to have a force of 26 kilotons, more powerful than the Nagasaki atomic bomb
58th Tony Awards: "Avenue Q" (musical) & "I Am My Own Wife" (play) win
Tamil is established as a Classical language by the President of India, Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam in a joint sitting of the two houses of the Indian Parliament.
The United States Supreme Court votes to ban medical marijuana in Gonzales v. Raich.
Stanley Cup Final, Honda Center, Anaheim, CA: Anaheim Ducks defeat Ottawa Senators, 6-2 for a 4-1 series win; Ducks' first Championship
Gustav Vasais elected King of Sweden, ending the Kalmar Union
Treaty of Vossem: French KingLouis XIVand Frederik Willem, Elector of Brandenburg
Alexis St. Martin shot in the stomach and treated by physicianWilliam Beaumonton Mackinac Island. Leads Beaumont to conduct digestion experiments through hole in St. Martin's stomach.
Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA) formed byGeorge Williamsin London
British Open Men's Golf, Muirfield: ScotsmanJames Braidwins the first of five Open titles by three strokes overHarry Vardonof Jersey
The death ofYuan Shikai, ruler of much of China since 1912, causes central government to virtually collapse in the face of pressure from warlords, and from political reformers includingSun Yat-Sen
Southwark Bridge in London is opened to traffic by KingGeorge Vand Queen Mary.
Edgar Wallacebecomes the first British radio sports journalist reporting on The Derby for the British Broadcasting Company (BBC)
Italian-American gangsterAlbert Anastasiais convicted of illegal possession of a firearm and sentenced to two years in prison
Walter Chryslerreorganizes the failing Maxwell Motor Company and founds automobile manufacturerChryslerCorporation in Detroit, Michigan
Sigmund Freudarrives in London, fleeing the Nazi annexation of Austria
French Prime MinisterCharles de Gaullesays Algeria will always be French
"TheSteve AllenShow" last airs on NBC-TV
The Beatles meet their producerGeorge Martinfor the first time, and record "Besame Mucho" withPete Beston drums
The Rolling Stonesrelease music single "(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction"
Civil rights activistJames Meredithwounded by white sniper in Mississippi
SenatorRobert F. Kennedydies from his wounds after he was shot the previous night
Joe Namathresigns from NFL afterPete Rozelle, football commissioner, said he must sell his stake in a bar
Final broadcast of "TheEd SullivanShow" (a repeat from 7 February) on CBS-TV
John LennonandYoko Onoappear for an unannounced performance, joiningFrank Zappaon stage at Fillmore East in NYC
Willie Mayshits record 22nd (& last) extra inning HR
Nolan Ryan's bid for 2nd no-hitter broken in 6th inning
The "Washington Post" reports the US has developed a neutron bomb
US TV news program "20/20", created byRoone Arledgepremieres on ABC
"Octopussy", 13th James Bond film, starringRoger Moore, Maud Adams and Louis Jourdan premieres in London
Li Xiannian becomes President of the People's Republic of China andDeng Xiaopingthe supreme commander
"Storms of Life" debut album byRandy Travisis released (Billboard Album of the Year, 1987)
French Open Women's Tennis: 17-year-old GermanSteffi Grafbeats World #1Martina Navratilova6-4, 4-6, 8-6; Graf's first Grand Slam victory
For 2nd time this season,Cecil Fielderbelts 3 home runs in a game
NBC announcesJay Lenowill succeedJohnny Carsonas host on "The Tonight Show" on May 25, 1992
NY Mets first basemanEddie Murrayrecords his 1,510th run batted in during 15-1 thrashing of Pittsburgh Pirates to pass Mickey Mantle as all-time RBI leader among MLB switch-hitters
West Indian cricket batsmanBrian Larahits world 1st-class record 501 not out and 390 runs in 1 day for Warwickshire vs Durham at Edgbaston; only quintuple-hundred in first-class history
TV sitcom "Sex and the City" premieres in the US on HBO, starringSarah Jessica Parker,Kim Cattrall, Kristin Davis andCynthia Nixon
"The Bourne Identity" directed by Doug Liman and starringMatt Damonpremieres in Los Angeles, California
10" of snowfall in New England, part of a "year without a summer" which followed the eruption of Mount Tambora in Indonesia
The eruption of Novarupta in Alaska begins, the largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century
Operation Overlord: As part of the D-Day landings, the 82nd Airborne Division arrives at the French town of Sainte-Mère-Église
Israeli troops occupy Gaza during second day of the Six-Day War
Wen Tianxiang, Prime Minister of China and scholar-general in the last years of the Southern Song Dynasty, born in Ji'an, China (d. 1283)
Regiomontanus [Johannes Müller von Königsberg], German mathematician and astronomer, prepares astronomical tables, born in Königsberg, Germany (d. 1476)

Giovanni Diodati, Swiss Protestant clergyman, born in Lucca, Republic of Lucca (now Italy) (d. 1649)
